message:
Update tests for async search and list commands

Updates test suites to work with the new async versions of list and search
commands. Also syncs beads tracking state.

## search.test.ts Changes

Added comprehensive tests for personal prompt search integration:

1. **Existing behavior preserved** (5 tests):
   - Basic search still works
   - Category/tag filters work
   - JSON output format correct
   - Score sorting maintained

2. **Authentication flag tests** (4 tests):
   - --mine requires authentication
   - --saved requires authentication
   - Proper error format in JSON mode
   - Correct exit codes

3. **Premium user tests** (3 tests mocked):
   - --all searches local + personal
   - Results properly merged
   - Source tags in output

4. **Test infrastructure**:
   - Mock fetch for API responses
   - Mock process.exit for exit code testing
   - Environment variable isolation
   - Console output capture

## json-schema-golden.test.ts Changes

Updated all list command tests from sync to async:
- `listCommand({ json: true })` → `await listCommand({ json: true })`
- All affected test cases now use async/await
- No schema changes - just async compatibility

Tests updated:
- "outputs an array of prompts"
- "each prompt has all required fields"
- "required fields have correct types"
- "optional fields have correct types when present"
- And others using listCommand

## Beads State

Syncs current task tracking state for the personal prompts feature work.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-94r0","title":"Add search integration for personal prompts in CLI","description":"Add search integration for personal prompts in CLI.

When user is authenticated with premium account, extend search to include:
- User's own prompts (if they have any)
- User's saved prompts
- User's collections (search collection names and descriptions)
- User's prompt notes

Search Flow:
1. Search local prompt registry first (fast, always available)
2. If authenticated, concurrently search premium API endpoints
3. Merge results, marking personal prompts with [personal] tag
4. Display combined results with source indicators

CLI Options:
- jfp search <query> --mine (search only personal prompts)
- jfp search <query> --saved (search only saved prompts)
- jfp search <query> --all (search everything, default for premium users)
- jfp search <query> --local (search only local registry, default for free users)

Graceful Degradation:
- If API unavailable, fall back to local-only search with warning
- If not authenticated, only search local registry (no warning)
","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T20:23:10.125806962-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T20:46:49.499747465-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-94r0","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-dio8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T20:24:48.874313433-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9g76","title":"Implement jfp save <prompt-id> command","description":"Implement jfp save command to save prompts to user's premium account.

Usage: jfp save <prompt-id>

Features:
- Requires premium authentication (prompts user to login if not authenticated)
- Validates prompt ID exists in local registry before saving
- POST to /api/cli/saved-prompts with { prompt_id }
- Displays success message with saved prompt title
- Handles duplicate saves gracefully (already saved message)
- Requires premium tier (show upgrade message for free users)

Error Handling:
- 401: Prompt to login
- 403: Display "Saving prompts requires premium subscription"
- 404: Display "Prompt not found"
- Network errors: Display connection error message

Output Format:
- Default: "Saved: [prompt title]"
- With --json: { saved: true, prompt_id, title, saved_at }
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T20:23:04.856764992-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T20:42:03.114273977-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T20:42:03.114273977-05:00","close_reason":"Command fully implemented with auth, premium validation, API integration, error handling, --json support. 8 tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9g76","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-dio8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T20:24:43.590423922-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9ho","title":"Initialize monorepo structure with Bun workspaces -- Background: This project uses a monorepo architecture with packages/core (shared prompts/search/exports), apps/web (Next.js 16), and CLI (jfp.ts). Using Bun workspaces enables dependency sharing between packages. The monorepo structure follows brennerbot.org patterns for consistency. This is foundational - all other work depends on it.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:26:50.541605041-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T01:16:55.442579082-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T01:16:55.442579082-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented monorepo structure, core package with types, registry, search engine (BM25, tokenizer, synonyms), and export functions. CLI is working."}
 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9is","title":"Build Next.js 16 web application (apps/web) -- Background: The web app provides human-friendly browsing of prompts with copy-to-clipboard, basket/export, and SpotlightSearch (Cmd+K). Built on Next.js 16 App Router + React 19, Tailwind CSS 4, shadcn/ui. Design patterns derived from brennerbot.org. Deployed on Vercel with Cloudflare DNS for jeffreysprompts.com domain.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:26:52.724612649-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T02:29:13.659713921-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T02:29:13.659713921-05:00","close_reason":"Web app fully implemented in apps/web/: Next.js 16 App Router, React 19, Tailwind CSS 4, shadcn/ui components. Features include copy-to-clipboard, basket/export, SpotlightSearch (Cmd+K), prompt filtering, BM25 search. Deployed on Vercel at jeffreysprompts.com.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9is","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-fnf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:37:00.274168829-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9r7s","title":"Add --json flag support for all new CLI commands","description":"Add --json flag support to all new CLI commands for machine-readable output.

Commands to Update:
- jfp login (output: { authenticated: true, email, tier })
- jfp logout (output: { logged_out: true })
- jfp whoami (output: { email, tier, user_id })
- jfp collections list (output: { collections: [...] })
- jfp collections show (output: { collection, items: [...] })
- jfp notes (output: { prompt_id, notes: [...] })
- jfp save (output: { saved: true, prompt_id })
- jfp list --mine/--saved (output: { prompts: [...] })

Implementation:
- Add --json/-j flag to all relevant commands
- When --json is set, output only valid JSON to stdout
- Errors should also be JSON: { error: true, message, code }
- Progress/status messages go to stderr when --json is enabled
- Ensure consistent field naming across all commands
","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T20:23:15.385023105-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T20:23:52.593189224-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9r7s","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-cgj5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T20:25:08.868781233-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9r7s","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9g76","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T20:25:19.154441027-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9r7s","title":"Add --json flag support for all new CLI commands","description":"Add --json flag support to all new CLI commands for machine-readable output.

Commands to Update:
- jfp login (output: { authenticated: true, email, tier })
- jfp logout (output: { logged_out: true })
- jfp whoami (output: { email, tier, user_id })
- jfp collections list (output: { collections: [...] })
- jfp collections show (output: { collection, items: [...] })
- jfp notes (output: { prompt_id, notes: [...] })
- jfp save (output: { saved: true, prompt_id })
- jfp list --mine/--saved (output: { prompts: [...] })

Implementation:
- Add --json/-j flag to all relevant commands
- When --json is set, output only valid JSON to stdout
- Errors should also be JSON: { error: true, message, code }
- Progress/status messages go to stderr when --json is enabled
- Ensure consistent field naming across all commands
","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T20:23:15.385023105-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T20:51:13.002314137-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9r7s","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-cgj5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T20:25:08.868781233-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9r7s","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9g76","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T20:25:19.154441027-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
